name: vscode-safe-exec
description: Best-effort guardrails for risky terminal commands, protected VS Code commands, suspicious large edits, and supported file operations.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Safe Exec can still show approval prompts and inspect editor changes in untrusted workspaces, but Workspace Trust is not a sandbox and guarded command coverage still depends on explicit proxy commands.

commands.title: Safe Exec: Open Recommended Proxy Keybindings
Safe Exec: Show Recent File Operations
Safe Exec: Restore Last Recoverable File Operation
Safe Exec: Browse Recoverable File Operations
Safe Exec: Proxy Run Selected Text In Terminal
Safe Exec: Proxy GitHub Copilot Generate

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Safe Exec
walkthroughs.description: Review the first-run guidance, wire proxy keybindings, and inspect recent Safe Exec activity.

steps.title: Review the onboarding guide
steps.description: Open the onboarding guide to see what Safe Exec protects, what it does not protect, and how Workspace Trust fits in.

steps.title: Review recommended proxy keybindings
steps.description: Open your user keybindings.json beside a recommended Safe Exec proxy snippet. Safe Exec does not edit keybindings automatically.

media.altText: Illustrative Safe Exec status and keybinding guidance

steps.title: Inspect the diff-based edit review flow
steps.description: Safe Exec rolls back suspicious edits first, then lets you review a real diff before reapplying.

steps.title: Open recent Safe Exec activity
steps.description: Review approvals, denials, degraded replays, file-operation recovery events, and related best-effort status history for this workspace.

steps.title: Review recoverable file operations
steps.description: Open recent best-effort file-operation history and restore options for supported delete and rename flows.

media.altText: Illustrative Safe Exec file operation recovery

safeExec.rulesPath.description: Workspace-relative or absolute path to the JSON rules file.

enumDescriptions: Protect common Node/web deploy and JavaScript toolchain surfaces.
Protect common Python packaging and environment surfaces.
Protect common Docker lifecycle and configuration surfaces.
Protect common Terraform, Helm, and Kubernetes mutation surfaces.
Protect common Git force-push and CI workflow surfaces.

safeExec.policyBundles.description: Opt-in Safe Exec policy bundle IDs. Built-in bundles include node-web, python, docker, terraform-kubernetes, and git-ci.

safeExec.protectedCommands.description: Additional command IDs that should require approval when executed through Safe Exec proxy commands.

safeExec.terminal.killStrategy.description: How Safe Exec attempts to stop a risky terminal command after shell integration reports it.

safeExec.editHeuristics.minChangedCharac.description: Minimum changed characters before Safe Exec asks for approval on edits.

safeExec.editHeuristics.minAffectedLines.description: Minimum affected lines before Safe Exec asks for approval on edits.

safeExec.editHeuristics.maxPreviewCharac.description: Legacy fallback preview size for suspicious edit review.
safeExec.editHeuristics.maxPreviewCharac.deprecationMessage: Safe Exec now uses a full diff view for suspicious edit review. This setting is retained for compatibility and no longer affects the primary review flow.

safeExec.fileOps.enabled.description: Enable Safe Exec best-effort file-operation evaluation and recovery for supported VS Code create, delete, and rename flows.

safeExec.fileOps.maxSnapshotBytes.description: Maximum bytes Safe Exec will capture per file snapshot before delete or rename. Larger files fall back to metadata-only recovery.

safeExec.fileOps.maxFilesPerOperation.description: Maximum files Safe Exec will snapshot for a single delete or rename operation before falling back to partial or metadata-only recovery.

safeExec.fileOps.minBulkOperationCount.description: Minimum affected file count before Safe Exec classifies a file operation as bulk.

safeExec.fileOps.protectedPathPatterns.description: Additional regex path patterns that should raise file-operation risk and snapshot priority.

safeExec.fileOps.ignoredPathPatterns.description: Additional regex path patterns Safe Exec should treat as lower-signal when evaluating file operations.

safeExec.fileOps.sensitiveExtensions.description: Additional file extensions that should raise file-operation risk and recovery priority.

safeExec.fileOps.sensitiveFileNames.description: Additional file names that should raise file-operation risk and recovery priority.

safeExec.fileOps.captureBinarySnapshots.description: Allow Safe Exec to capture byte-for-byte snapshots for small binary files. When disabled, binary files fall back to metadata-only recovery.
